# Locked Experimental Protocol — TCC iTransformer Regime Detection

**Version:** 1.0  
**Date:** 2026-05-30  
**Status:** FROZEN — no changes without explicit sign-off in revision_log.md  
**Author:** Agent 1 (code/experiment agent)  
**For:** Agent 2 (writing agent) — do not edit this file; treat as ground truth for all numbers  

---

## 1. Purpose

This document locks every methodological decision made prior to computing final
test-set results. It exists to make re-running, auditing, and peer-review
straightforward by fully specifying the pipeline before revealing the outcome.

---

## 2. Data

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Vintage | FRED-MD 2026-04 (released April 2026) |
| Raw series | 128 monthly series |
| Balanced cutoff (missingness filter) | Drop any series with >50% NaN before 1965-01; retain 122 series |
| Panel file | `data/raw/fred_md_transformed_balanced_2026_04.parquet` |
| Preprocessing | McCracken & Ng (2016) transformation codes; winsorize at 5σ on TRAIN; standardize (z-score) using TRAIN mean/std |
| Date range after filter | 1959-03 to 2026-04 (but TRAIN starts 1965-01 due to warm-up) |

### USREC ground truth

NBER USREC snapshot: `data/snapshots/nber_usrec.csv`  
Source: FRED series `USREC` (downloaded 2026-04)

---

## 3. Locked Train / Validation / Test Split — B1

**This split is pre-registered and IMMUTABLE. No re-splitting allowed.**

| Split | Months | Period |
|---|---|---|
| TRAIN | 420 | 1965-01 to 1999-12 |
| VAL | 120 | 2000-01 to 2009-12 |
| TEST | 194 | 2010-01 to 2026-04 |

**NBER recession months in TEST:** 2020-03, 2020-04 (n=2 months)  
**NBER recession months in VAL:** 2001-03 to 2001-11 (dot-com, 9 months), 2007-12 to 2009-06 (GFC, 19 months) = 28 months total

**Rationale for B1:** Train on the post-1965 stable era (avoids 1950s data
quality issues). VAL covers two full NBER cycles (dot-com, GFC). TEST is
exclusively out-of-sample from 2010 onwards, containing only the COVID shock.

---

## 4. Encoder Architecture — iTransformer B1 Winner

| Hyperparameter | Value | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Window size W | 6 | Stage-2 sweep winner (W6_d7_K4) |
| Latent dimension d_lat | 7 | Stage-2 sweep winner |
| Number of clusters K | 4 | Stage-2 sweep winner |
| Encoder type | iTransformer (SSL, trainable) | Pre-registered |
| Dropout | 0.20 | Stage-1 sweep winner (lr1e-03_drop20) |
| Learning rate | 1e-3 | Stage-1 sweep winner |
| Batch size | 1 | Per config |
| Epochs (max) | 72 (early stop at epoch 72) | MLflow run |
| SageMaker job | itransformer-1777581449-0d38 | Cached embeddings used |

Encoder taxonomy: `trainable_ssl` (NOT "foundation model" — see revision_log).

---

## 5. Downstream Pipeline — Cells

6 primary downstream cells (all reported):

| Cell | DR | Clusterer |
|---|---|---|
| pca_kmeans | PCA(2) | KMeans(K=4, seed=42) |
| pca_hdbscan | PCA(2) | HDBSCAN (auto min_cluster_size via DBCV optimization) |
| umap_kmeans | UMAP(2, seed=42) | KMeans(K=4, seed=42) |
| umap_hdbscan | UMAP(2, seed=42) | HDBSCAN |
| tsne_kmeans | t-SNE(2, seed=42, init=pca) | KMeans(K=4, seed=42) |
| tsne_hdbscan | t-SNE(2, seed=42, init=pca) | HDBSCAN |

1 supplementary cell: `pca_gmm` (GMM K=4, covariance=full; for Appendix only).

---

## 6. LOCKED Recession-Cluster Selection Rule (Finding #2 fix)

**CRITICAL: This is the key protocol change relative to prior runs.**

**Old (biased) rule:** pick the cluster with highest NBER overlap on TEST.  
**New (locked) rule:** VAL-only assignment, frozen before TEST is examined.

### Step-by-step

1. Fit DR (PCA/UMAP/t-SNE) **on TRAIN** only. Transform VAL.
2. Fit clusterer (KMeans/HDBSCAN) **on TRAIN** only. Predict **VAL** labels.
3. Call `fit_nber_assignment(val_labels, val_dates, usrec)` to derive the
   frozen assignment dict `{cluster_id → 0 or 1}`. This uses NBER on VAL
   (2000–2009), which is legitimate model selection (no TEST contamination).
4. Freeze the mapping. Apply verbatim to TEST via `nber_overlap_frozen`.

### Notes

- The `fit_nber_assignment` function already implements a 2-month lag tolerance
  and a ≥2× enrichment factor over base rate (see `regime_validation.py`).
- If no cluster passes the enrichment threshold on VAL, the highest-recession-
  share cluster is used (fallback in `fit_nber_assignment`).
- For HDBSCAN VAL prediction: `hdbscan.approximate_predict` is used
  (requires `prediction_data=True` during fit — set in clustering_ablation).
- t-SNE has no `transform()`: fit on TRAIN∪VAL∪TEST jointly (since t-SNE is
  used only for visualization, not out-of-sample prediction). VAL labels come
  from the joint fit applied to the VAL portion.

---

## 7. LOCKED Best-Cell Selection Rule (Finding #2 fix)

**Old (biased) rule:** inspect TEST C1 table, pick highest NBER F1 cell.  
**New (locked) rule:** select on VAL, report TEST.

Best cell = `argmax over 6 cells of mean(NBER_F1_val, Sahm_F1_val, CFNAI_F1_val)`.  
Ties broken by VAL silhouette score (higher = better).

**For the canonical comparison table (encoder × cell):** report all 6 cells
separately. The "best cell" row appears as the primary row in the thesis table,
with all 6 cells in the appendix.

---

## 8. Metrics Panel (7 metrics)

| Column | Description | Set |
|---|---|---|
| `dbcv` | DBCV (Moulavi et al. 2014), validity_index from hdbscan lib; NaN for KMeans | TEST |
| `n_clusters_test` | Count of unique non-noise clusters | TEST |
| `noise_fraction_test` | Fraction of TEST points labeled -1 | TEST |
| `nber_f1` | F1 of frozen VAL-derived assignment applied to TEST | TEST |
| `nber_f1_legacy_maxF1` | Post-hoc max-F1 (bias witness only, not cited in text) | TEST |
| `bai_perron_f1` | Agreement of cluster transitions with PELT breaks on PC1 of test signal | TEST |
| `crisis_window_coverage` | Fraction of canonical crises (dot-com 2001, GFC 2007-09, COVID 2020) whose dominant cluster maps to recession | TEST |

### C1 External Validity (multi-label panel)

4 external label series, compared via F1 on TEST (with locked assignment from VAL):

| Label | Series | Recession threshold | Direction |
|---|---|---|---|
| `chauvet_piger` | Chauvet-Piger (2008) Markov-Switching | >0.5 | above |
| `sahm` | Sahm Rule real-time indicator (Sahm 2019) | >0.5 | above |
| `cfnai_ma3` | CFNAI 3-month moving average | <-0.70 | below |
| `oecd_cli` | OECD composite leading indicator (binary) | >0.5 | above |

AUC-ROC in C1 table: computed as balanced accuracy = (TPR + TNR) / 2 from the
binary cluster prediction (NOT from the raw indicator series — see revision_log
for the sign-orientation bug fix).

### C3 Structural Breaks — PELT (NOT Bai-Perron)

**CORRECTION:** The code uses `ruptures.Pelt(model='l2', pen=10, min_size=12)`,
NOT the Bai-Perron (1998) iterative OLS procedure. All outputs previously labeled
"bai_perron_f1" or "Bai-Perron F1" should be read as "PELT F1" in the thesis.

Bai-Perron (1998) requires `statsmodels.stats.breaktest` (not available in the
current codebase). The PELT L2 algorithm is a valid multi-break detector and is
appropriate for this application; the naming was incorrect.

F1 computed as: cluster-label transitions vs PELT break dates, ±3-month tolerance.

Headline series: INDPRO, PAYEMS, UNRATE.

### C4 Bootstrap Stability

- Algorithm: Ben-Hur et al. (2002) resampling stability
- Resamples: n=100, resample_frac=0.8
- Metric: Jaccard similarity of cluster membership across resamples (per cluster)
- Summary: ARI across all clusters; per-cluster: `mean_jaccard`, `std_jaccard`, `Pr(J≥0.60)`
- Stable cluster: `Pr(J≥0.60) > 0.50` (50th percentile above threshold)

`Pr(J≥0.60)` is approximated via the normal CDF: `1 - Φ((0.60 - mean_J) / std_J)`
(normal approximation; raw Jaccard resamples are not cached).

---

## 9. Falsification Gate (Phase B2)

Pre-registered gate: if any simpler encoder achieves DBCV ≥ iTransformer + 0.05
on the same B1 split and same downstream cell, iTransformer is rejected as the
primary geometric pipeline.

**Gate status: FIRED**

| Encoder | TEST DBCV |
|---|---|
| iTransformer B1 (pca_hdbscan) | 0.1656 |
| MLP-AE | 0.7892 |
| SVD | 0.6656 |
| Linear-AE | 0.2529 |

Conclusion: iTransformer does NOT produce superior geometric structure
(DBCV) compared to simple baselines. The thesis primary finding is this
falsification, not iTransformer dominance.
